#### Sen uzman bir Full-Stack Geliştiricisin. Hedefimiz, modern ve ölçeklenebilir bir web teknolojisi yığını kullanarak bir Notion klonu MVP'si (Minimum Viable Product) inşa etmek. Lütfen kodu yazmadan önce mimariyi düşün ve dosyaları modüler bir şekilde oluştur.  
####   
#### **Kullanılacak Teknoloji Yığını:**  
#### * **Framework:** Next.js (App Router) ile React  
#### * **Dil:** TypeScript  
#### * **Stil:** Tailwind CSS ve Lucide-React (ikonlar için)  
#### * **Veritabanı ve ORM:** Prisma ve yerel SQLite (geliştirme kolaylığı için)  
#### * **Editör:** TipTap (veya BlockNote - blok tabanlı zengin metin editörü için)  
####   
#### **Veritabanı Şeması (Prisma):**  
#### Öncelikle `prisma/schema.prisma` dosyasını oluştur ve aşağıdaki ilişkiye sahip bir `Document` (veya Page) modeli kur:  
#### * `id` (String, UUID)  
#### * `title` (String)  
#### * `content` (String/JSON - editör içeriği için)  
#### * `isPublished` (Boolean, default false)  
#### * `parentDocumentId` (String, nullable - iç içe sayfalar (nested pages) için kendi kendine referans veren ilişki)  
#### * `createdAt` ve `updatedAt`  
####   
#### **Aşama 1 - Temel Hedefler (Şu an yapman gerekenler):**  
#### 1.  **Veritabanı Kurulumu:** Prisma şemasını oluştur ve SQLite veritabanını başlat (`npx prisma db push` işleminin yapılabilir olduğunu varsay).  
#### 2.  **Layout ve UI İskeleti:** Tüm uygulamayı kapsayan ana bir düzen (layout) oluştur.   
####     * **Sol Kenar Çubuğu (Sidebar):** Daraltılabilir (collapsible) olmalı. İçerisinde mevcut dokümanların ağaç yapısında (hiyerarşik) listesi ve "Yeni Doküman Ekle" butonu bulunmalı.  
####     * **Ana İçerik Alanı:** Seçilen dokümanın başlığının (`h1` büyüklüğünde) ve altında editörün yer alacağı boş bir tuval alanı.  
#### 3.  **Temel API Route'ları:** Next.js App Router standartlarına uygun olarak doküman oluşturma (POST), listeleme (GET), getirme (GET /id), güncelleme (PATCH) ve silme (DELETE) işlemlerini yapacak API uçlarını (endpoints) yaz.  
#### 4.  **Editör Entegrasyonu:** Ana içerik alanına basit bir TipTap (veya seçtiğin blok editör) entegre et. İçerik değiştiğinde otomatik kaydetme (auto-save) veya bir "Kaydet" butonu mantığı için temel bir debounce fonksiyonu hazırla.  
####   
#### Lütfen bu mimariyi tek seferde, eksiksiz ve çalışır bir kod tabanı olarak adım adım inşa et. İlk olarak Prisma şemasını ve klasör yapısını kurarak başla.

Let me think about each file:

**prisma/schema.prisma**:
```prisma
generator client {
  provider = "prisma-client-js"
}

datasource db {
  provider = "sqlite"
  url      = env("DATABASE_URL")
}

model Document {
  id                String     @id @default(uuid())
  title             String     @default("Untitled")
  content           String     @default("")
  isPublished       Boolean    @default(false)
  parentDocumentId  String?
  parentDocument    Document?  @relation("DocumentHierarchy", fields: [parentDocumentId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  childDocuments    Document[] @relation("DocumentHierarchy")
  createdAt         DateTime   @default(now())
  updatedAt         DateTime   @updatedAt
}
```

**.env**:
```
DATABASE_URL="file:./dev.db"
```

**package.json**: Need to include all the dependencies.

**src/lib/prisma.ts**: Singleton pattern for Prisma client.

**src/lib/utils.ts**: cn helper for tailwind, tree builder.

**src/hooks/useDebounce.ts**: Custom hook.

**src/app/layout.tsx**: Root layout.

**src/app/globals.css**: Tailwind imports.

**src/app/page.tsx**: Home page - list documents, redirect to first or new.

- `AppShell.tsx`: Top-level layout with sidebar + main
- `Sidebar.tsx`: Recursive document tree
- `DocumentItem.tsx`: Single tree node (recursive)
- `DocumentEditor.tsx`: Editor view for selected document

**src/lib/prisma.ts**:
```ts
import { PrismaClient } from "@prisma/client";

const globalForPrisma = global as unknown as { prisma: PrismaClient };

export const prisma = globalForPrisma.prisma || new PrismaClient();

if (process.env.NODE_ENV !== "production") globalForPrisma.prisma = prisma;
```

**src/lib/utils.ts**:
```ts
import { type ClassValue, clsx } from "clsx";
import { twMerge } from "tailwind-merge";

export function cn(...inputs: ClassValue[]) {
  return twMerge(clsx(inputs));
}
```

Wait, I need to be careful about which dependencies to include. `clsx` and `tailwind-merge` are common.

**src/hooks/useDebounce.ts**:
```ts
"use client";

import { useEffect, useState } from "react";

export function useDebounce<T>(value: T, delay: number = 500): T {
  const [debouncedValue, setDebouncedValue] = useState<T>(value);

  useEffect(() => {
    const timer = setTimeout(() => setDebouncedValue(value), delay);
    return () => clearTimeout(timer);
  }, [value, delay]);

  return debouncedValue;
}
```

**src/app/api/documents/route.ts**:
```ts
import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from "next/server";
import { prisma } from "@/lib/prisma";

export async function GET() {
  try {
    const documents = await prisma.document.findMany({
      orderBy: { updatedAt: "desc" },
    });
    return NextResponse.json(documents);
  } catch (error) {
    return NextResponse.json({ error: "Failed to fetch documents" }, { status: 500 });
  }
}

export async function POST(req: NextRequest) {
  try {
    const body = await req.json();
    const { title, content, parentDocumentId, isPublished } = body;
    const document = await prisma.document.create({
      data: {
        title: title ?? "Untitled",
        content: content ?? "",
        parentDocumentId: parentDocumentId ?? null,
        isPublished: isPublished ?? false,
      },
    });
    return NextResponse.json(document, { status: 201 });
  } catch (error) {
    return NextResponse.json({ error: "Failed to create document" }, { status: 500 });
  }
}
```

**src/app/api/documents/[id]/route.ts**:
```ts
import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from "next/server";
import { prisma } from "@/lib/prisma";

export async function GET(_req: NextRequest, { params }: { params: { id: string } }) {
  try {
    const document = await prisma.document.findUnique({
      where: { id: params.id },
    });
    if (!document) {
      return NextResponse.json({ error: "Document not found" }, { status: 404 });
    }
    return NextResponse.json(document);
  } catch (error) {
    return NextResponse.json({ error: "Failed to fetch document" }, { status: 500 });
  }
}

export async function PATCH(req: NextRequest, { params }: { params: { id: string } }) {
  try {
    const body = await req.json();
    const document = await prisma.document.update({
      where: { id: params.id },
      data: body,
    });
    return NextResponse.json(document);
  } catch (error) {
    return NextResponse.json({ error: "Failed to update document" }, { status: 500 });
  }
}

export async function DELETE(_req: NextRequest, { params }: { params: { id: string } }) {
  try {
    await prisma.document.delete({
      where: { id: params.id },
    });
    return NextResponse.json({ success: true });
  } catch (error) {
    return NextResponse.json({ error: "Failed to delete document" }, { status: 500 });
  }
}
```

import "./globals.css";

export const metadata: Metadata = {

};

export default function RootLayout({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) {

    </html>

}

**src/components/AppShell.tsx**:
This is the main client component that holds sidebar state and selected document state.

```tsx
"use client";

import { useState, useEffect, useCallback } from "react";
import { Sidebar } from "./Sidebar";
import { DocumentEditor } from "./DocumentEditor";
import { EmptyState } from "./EmptyState";
import type { Document } from "@/lib/types";

export function AppShell() {
  const [documents, setDocuments] = useState<Document[]>([]);
  const [selectedId, setSelectedId] = useState<string | null>(null);
  const [sidebarOpen, setSidebarOpen] = useState(true);
  const [isLoading, setIsLoading] = useState(true);

  const fetchDocuments = useCallback(async () => {
    try {
      const res = await fetch("/api/documents");
      const data = await res.json();
      setDocuments(data);
    } catch (e) {
      console.error(e);
    } finally {
      setIsLoading(false);
    }
  }, []);

  useEffect(() => {
    fetchDocuments();
  }, [fetchDocuments]);

  const selected = documents.find((d) => d.id === selectedId) ?? null;

  return (
    <div className="flex h-screen w-full">
      <Sidebar
        documents={documents}
        selectedId={selectedId}
        onSelect={setSelectedId}
        onChange={fetchDocuments}
        isOpen={sidebarOpen}
        onToggle={() => setSidebarOpen((v) => !v)}
      />
      <main className="flex-1 overflow-hidden">
        {selected ? (
          <DocumentEditor
            document={selected}
            onChange={fetchDocuments}
          />
        ) : (
          <EmptyState onCreated={fetchDocuments} />
        )}
      </main>
    </div>
  );
}
```

Hmm, let me think about types. I'll create a types file.

**src/lib/types.ts**:
```ts
export interface Document {
  id: string;
  title: string;
  content: string;
  isPublished: boolean;
  parentDocumentId: string | null;
  createdAt: string;
  updatedAt: string;
}

export interface DocumentNode extends Document {
  children: DocumentNode[];
}
```

**src/lib/tree.ts**:
```ts
import type { Document, DocumentNode } from "./types";

export function buildTree(documents: Document[]): DocumentNode[] {
  const map = new Map<string, DocumentNode>();
  documents.forEach((doc) => map.set(doc.id, { ...doc, children: [] }));
  const roots: DocumentNode[] = [];
  map.forEach((node) => {
    if (node.parentDocumentId && map.has(node.parentDocumentId)) {
      map.get(node.parentDocumentId)!.children.push(node);
    } else {
      roots.push(node);
    }
  });
  const sortRec = (nodes: DocumentNode[]) => {
    nodes.sort((a, b) => a.title.localeCompare(b.title));
    nodes.forEach((n) => sortRec(n.children));
  };
  sortRec(roots);
  return roots;
}
```

**src/components/Sidebar.tsx**:
```tsx
"use client";

import { useState } from "react";
import { ChevronLeft, ChevronRight, FileText, Plus } from "lucide-react";
import { DocumentTree } from "./DocumentTree";
import type { Document } from "@/lib/types";
import { cn } from "@/lib/utils";

interface Props {
  documents: Document[];
  selectedId: string | null;
  onSelect: (id: string) => void;
  onChange: () => void;
  isOpen: boolean;
  onToggle: () => void;
}

export function Sidebar({ documents, selectedId, onSelect, onChange, isOpen, onToggle }: Props) {
  const createRoot = async () => {
    const res = await fetch("/api/documents", {
      method: "POST",
      headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
      body: JSON.stringify({ title: "Untitled" }),
    });
    const doc = await res.json();
    onChange();
    onSelect(doc.id);
  };

  return (
    <aside
      className={cn(
        "flex h-full flex-col border-r border-gray-200 bg-gray-50 transition-all",
        isOpen ? "w-72" : "w-12"
      )}
    >
      <div className="flex items-center justify-between border-b border-gray-200 p-2">
        {isOpen && (
          <div className="flex items-center gap-2 px-1 text-sm font-semibold text-gray-700">
            <FileText className="h-4 w-4" />
            <span>My Workspace</span>
          </div>
        )}
        <button
          onClick={onToggle}
          className="ml-auto rounded p-1 text-gray-500 hover:bg-gray-200"
          aria-label="Toggle sidebar"
        >
          {isOpen ? <ChevronLeft className="h-4 w-4" /> : <ChevronRight className="h-4 w-4" />}
        </button>
      </div>

      {isOpen && (
        <>
          <div className="p-2">
            <button
              onClick={createRoot}
              className="flex w-full items-center gap-2 rounded px-2 py-1.5 text-sm text-gray-700 hover:bg-gray-200"
            >
              <Plus className="h-4 w-4" />
              <span>New Page</span>
            </button>
          </div>
          <div className="flex-1 overflow-y-auto px-1">
            <DocumentTree
              documents={documents}
              selectedId={selectedId}
              onSelect={onSelect}
              onChange={onChange}
            />
          </div>
        </>
      )}
    </aside>
  );
}
```

**src/components/DocumentTree.tsx** (recursive):
```tsx
"use client";

import { ChevronDown, ChevronRight, FileText, MoreHorizontal, Plus, Trash } from "lucide-react";
import { useState } from "react";
import type { Document, DocumentNode } from "@/lib/types";
import { buildTree } from "@/lib/tree";
import { cn } from "@/lib/utils";

interface Props {
  documents: Document[];
  selectedId: string | null;
  onSelect: (id: string) => void;
  onChange: () => void;
}

export function DocumentTree(props: Props) {
  const tree = buildTree(props.documents);
  if (tree.length === 0) {
    return <div className="px-2 py-2 text-xs text-gray-400">No pages yet</div>;
  }
  return (
    <ul>
      {tree.map((node) => (
        <TreeNode key={node.id} node={node} {...props} depth={0} />
      ))}
    </ul>
  );
}

function TreeNode({
  node,
  selectedId,
  onSelect,
  onChange,
  depth,
}: {
  node: DocumentNode;
  selectedId: string | null;
  onSelect: (id: string) => void;
  onChange: () => void;
  depth: number;
}) {
  const [open, setOpen] = useState(true);
  const [menuOpen, setMenuOpen] = useState(false);

  const addChild = async () => {
    const res = await fetch("/api/documents", {
      method: "POST",
      headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
      body: JSON.stringify({ title: "Untitled", parentDocumentId: node.id }),
    });
    const doc = await res.json();
    onChange();
    onSelect(doc.id);
  };

  const remove = async () => {
    if (!confirm(`Delete "${node.title}"?`)) return;
    await fetch(`/api/documents/${node.id}`, { method: "DELETE" });
    onChange();
  };

  return (
    <li>
      <div
        className={cn(
          "group flex items-center gap-1 rounded px-1 py-1 text-sm hover:bg-gray-200",
          selectedId === node.id && "bg-gray-200"
        )}
        style={{ paddingLeft: depth * 12 + 4 }}
      >
        <button
          onClick={() => setOpen((v) => !v)}
          className="text-gray-500"
        >
          {node.children.length > 0 ? (
            open ? <ChevronDown className="h-3 w-3" /> : <ChevronRight className="h-3 w-3" />
          ) : (
            <span className="block h-3 w-3" />
          )}
        </button>
        <button
          onClick={() => onSelect(node.id)}
          className="flex flex-1 items-center gap-1 truncate text-left text-gray-700"
        >
          <FileText className="h-3 w-3 shrink-0" />
          <span className="truncate">{node.title || "Untitled"}</span>
        </button>
        <div className="relative">
          <button
            onClick={(e) => {
              e.stopPropagation();
              setMenuOpen((v) => !v);
            }}
            className="hidden rounded p-0.5 text-gray-500 hover:bg-gray-300 group-hover:block"
          >
            <MoreHorizontal className="h-3 w-3" />
          </button>
          {menuOpen && (
            <div
              className="absolute right-0 z-10 mt-1 w-32 rounded border border-gray-200 bg-white py-1 text-xs shadow"
              onMouseLeave={() => setMenuOpen(false)}
            >
              <button
                onClick={(e) => {
                  e.stopPropagation();
                  setMenuOpen(false);
                  addChild();
                }}
                className="flex w-full items-center gap-2 px-2 py-1 hover:bg-gray-100"
              >
                <Plus className="h-3 w-3" /> Add sub-page
              </button>
              <button
                onClick={(e) => {
                  e.stopPropagation();
                  setMenuOpen(false);
                  remove();
                }}
                className="flex w-full items-center gap-2 px-2 py-1 text-red-600 hover:bg-gray-100"
              >
                <Trash className="h-3 w-3" /> Delete
              </button>
            </div>
          )}
        </div>
        <button
          onClick={(e) => {
            e.stopPropagation();
            addChild();
          }}
          className="hidden rounded p-0.5 text-gray-500 hover:bg-gray-300 group-hover:block"
        >
          <Plus className="h-3 w-3" />
        </button>
      </div>
      {open && node.children.length > 0 && (
        <ul>
          {node.children.map((c) => (
            <TreeNode
              key={c.id}
              node={c}
              selectedId={selectedId}
              onSelect={onSelect}
              onChange={onChange}
              depth={depth + 1}
            />
          ))}
        </ul>
      )}
    </li>
  );
}
```

**src/components/DocumentEditor.tsx**:
```tsx
"use client";

import { useEffect, useState } from "react";
import { Editor } from "./Editor";
import type { Document } from "@/lib/types";
import { useDebounce } from "@/hooks/useDebounce";

interface Props {
  document: Document;
  onChange: () => void;
}

export function DocumentEditor({ document, onChange }: Props) {
  const [title, setTitle] = useState(document.title);
  const [content, setContent] = useState(document.content);
  const [status, setStatus] = useState<"saved" | "saving" | "idle">("idle");

  useEffect(() => {
    setTitle(document.title);
    setContent(document.content);
    setStatus("saved");
  }, [document.id]);

  const debouncedTitle = useDebounce(title, 600);
  const debouncedContent = useDebounce(content, 600);

  useEffect(() => {
    if (debouncedTitle === document.title && debouncedContent === document.content) return;
    setStatus("saving");
    fetch(`/api/documents/${document.id}`, {
      method: "PATCH",
      headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
      body: JSON.stringify({ title: debouncedTitle, content: debouncedContent }),
    })
      .then(() => {
        setStatus("saved");
        onChange();
      })
      .catch(() => setStatus("idle"));
  }, [debouncedTitle, debouncedContent]);

  return (
    <div className="mx-auto flex h-full max-w-3xl flex-col">
      <div className="flex items-center justify-between px-12 pt-12">
        <div className="text-xs text-gray-400">
          {status === "saving" ? "Saving..." : status === "saved" ? "Saved" : ""}
        </div>
      </div>
      <input
        value={title}
        onChange={(e) => {
          setTitle(e.target.value);
          setStatus("idle");
        }}
        placeholder="Untitled"
        className="mx-12 mb-4 mt-2 bg-transparent text-4xl font-bold text-gray-900 outline-none placeholder:text-gray-300"
      />
      <div className="flex-1 overflow-y-auto px-12 pb-12">
        <Editor value={content} onChange={setContent} />
      </div>
    </div>
  );
}
```

**src/components/Editor.tsx** (TipTap):
```tsx
"use client";

import { useEditor, EditorContent } from "@tiptap/react";
import StarterKit from "@tiptap/starter-kit";
import { useEffect } from "react";

interface Props {
  value: string;
  onChange: (val: string) => void;
}

export function Editor({ value, onChange }: Props) {
  const editor = useEditor({
    extensions: [StarterKit],
    content: value || "",
    immediatelyRender: false,
    editorProps: {
      attributes: {
        class: "prose prose-sm sm:prose lg:prose-lg focus:outline-none min-h-[300px] max-w-none",
      },
    },
    onUpdate({ editor }) {
      onChange(editor.getHTML());
    },
  });

  useEffect(() => {
    if (editor && value !== editor.getHTML()) {
      editor.commands.setContent(value || "", false);
    }
  }, [value, editor]);

  return <EditorContent editor={editor} />;
}
```

"use client";

import { FileText } from "lucide-react";

interface Props {
  onCreated: () => void;
}

export function EmptyState({ onCreated }: Props) {
  const create = async () => {
    const res = await fetch("/api/documents", {
      method: "POST",
      headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
      body: JSON.stringify({ title: "Untitled" }),
    });
    const doc = await res.json();
    onCreated();
    if (typeof window !== "undefined") {
      window.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent("select-doc", { detail: doc.id }));
    }
  };

  return (
    <div className="flex h-full flex-col items-center justify-center text-gray-400">
      <FileText className="mb-3 h-12 w-12" />
      <p className="mb-4 text-sm">No page selected</p>
      <button
        onClick={create}
        className="rounded bg-blue-600 px-3 py-1.5 text-sm text-white hover:bg-blue-700"
      >
        Create a new page
      </button>
    </div>
  );
}
```

Let me also think about edge cases:
- When user deletes the currently selected document
- When the document content update comes from server and we don't want to overwrite user's in-flight edits

For simplicity, when the doc is re-fetched and selected doc changes, we reset the editor. But we don't want to refetch on every save... 

In my current `DocumentEditor`, the parent re-fetches docs on save (via `onChange()`). This causes the document reference to update, which triggers the useEffect that resets title/content. This could cause a flicker or save loop.

Let me think... When the user types, debouncedTitle and debouncedContent change, then we PATCH, then we call onChange which refetches documents. The new document has the new title/content. Then in the useEffect [document.id], we reset title/content to document values - but they're the same. So no infinite loop. But the `setStatus("saved")` is called, which is fine.

However, the value comparison in the second useEffect:
```js
if (debouncedTitle === document.title && debouncedContent === document.content) return;
```
This needs to use the *current* document prop, but when the parent re-renders with new data, document is the new reference. So the comparison is correct - if our debounced values match what's now in the document, we don't save.

But there's a subtle issue: between the time the user types and the debounce fires, the document prop may not have changed. So we save the user's new content. Good.

After save, we call onChange(), parent re-fetches, document updates, useEffect[document.id] doesn't fire (same ID), but the new document prop has new content. The save loop is broken because the debounced values match the document.

OK this should work. Let me also add a small optimization: don't reset on every document prop change, only on document.id change. That's what I have.

Alright, let me also handle the case where the user deletes the currently selected document. In TreeNode.remove, we delete and call onChange. The parent refetches, and the selected doc is no longer in the list, so selected becomes null. That should work.

But actually in my AppShell, the `selected` is computed from the documents array on each render. When the document is deleted, `selected` becomes null, and EmptyState is shown.

Let me finalize the AppShell:

```tsx
"use client";

import { useState, useEffect, useCallback } from "react";
import { Sidebar } from "./Sidebar";
import { DocumentEditor } from "./DocumentEditor";
import { EmptyState } from "./EmptyState";
import type { Document } from "@/lib/types";

export function AppShell() {
  const [documents, setDocuments] = useState<Document[]>([]);
  const [selectedId, setSelectedId] = useState<string | null>(null);
  const [sidebarOpen, setSidebarOpen] = useState(true);
  const [isLoading, setIsLoading] = useState(true);

  const fetchDocuments = useCallback(async () => {
    try {
      const res = await fetch("/api/documents");
      const data = await res.json();
      setDocuments(data);
    } catch (e) {
      console.error(e);
    } finally {
      setIsLoading(false);
    }
  }, []);

  useEffect(() => {
    fetchDocuments();
  }, [fetchDocuments]);

  const createDocument = useCallback(
    async (parentDocumentId: string | null = null) => {
      const res = await fetch("/api/documents", {
        method: "POST",
        headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
        body: JSON.stringify({ title: "Untitled", parentDocumentId }),
      });
      const doc = await res.json();
      await fetchDocuments();
      setSelectedId(doc.id);
    },
    [fetchDocuments]
  );

  const selected = documents.find((d) => d.id === selectedId) ?? null;

  return (
    <div className="flex h-screen w-full overflow-hidden">
      <Sidebar
        documents={documents}
        selectedId={selectedId}
        onSelect={setSelectedId}
        onCreate={createDocument}
        isOpen={sidebarOpen}
        onToggle={() => setSidebarOpen((v) => !v)}
      />
      <main className="flex-1 overflow-hidden">
        {selected ? (
          <DocumentEditor document={selected} onChange={fetchDocuments} />
        ) : (
          <EmptyState onCreate={() => createDocument(null)} />
        )}
      </main>
    </div>
  );
}
```

Now the Sidebar and EmptyState use `onCreate`.

One concern: the `Editor` component receives `value` (string) and `onChange`. When the parent re-renders with a new value, we use setContent. But this could cause an infinite loop if the editor's getHTML() doesn't match exactly what we pass in (e.g., whitespace differences). The `editor.commands.setContent(value || "", false)` second arg `false` means don't emit update event, which prevents the onChange. But then we still have a useEffect that does this on every value change... 

The flow:
1. User types -> editor.onUpdate -> onChange(html) -> parent state changes
2. Parent passes new value to Editor prop
3. Editor useEffect fires: compares value !== editor.getHTML() -> calls setContent
4. setContent with `false` doesn't emit update, so no onChange
5. The new value matches what was just typed, so the comparison is false on next render? No, the comparison in useEffect happens before the next render, so this is fine.

Actually wait: in the useEffect, the value is the new value, and editor.getHTML() is the editor's current content. If they differ (which they do because parent re-rendered with the new content), we set content. Then on the next render cycle, useEffect runs again with the same value (since props didn't change), and editor.getHTML() now matches, so we skip.

But the useEffect dep is [value, editor]. value didn't change, editor didn't change, so it doesn't re-run. Good.

But if the user types again, the new value is set in state, the prop changes, useEffect runs. Now value !== editor.getHTML() (because editor was updated immediately by TipTap). Wait, actually, when TipTap updates, it calls onUpdate which calls onChange which updates parent state. The new value passed back as prop might be the same as editor.getHTML() since they came from the same source. Hmm.

Actually let's trace:
1. User types "abc" -> TipTap editor content = "abc" -> editor.getHTML() = "<p>abc</p>" -> onUpdate emits -> onChange("<p>abc</p>") -> parent setContent("<p>abc</p>") -> re-render -> Editor gets value = "<p>abc</p>"
2. useEffect runs: value ("<p>abc</p>") === editor.getHTML() ("<p>abc</p>") -> skip setContent. 

When loading a new document:
1. document.id changes -> DocumentEditor useEffect runs -> setTitle, setContent with new document values
2. Editor gets new value prop
3. useEffect: value !== editor.getHTML() (old content) -> setContent with `false` (no emit) -> editor content updated -> re-render
4. useEffect deps [value, editor] - value didn't change in this re-render, editor ref didn't change -> doesn't re-run. 

But wait, in step 3, after setContent, the editor's content has changed, but the editor reference is the same. useEffect doesn't re-run. Good.

Actually wait, let me double check the Next.js route handler signatures. In Next.js 14:

```ts
export async function GET(
  request: NextRequest,
  { params }: { params: { id: string } }
) { ... }
```

Or in Next.js 15+:
```ts
export async function GET(
  request: NextRequest,
  { params }: { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }
) { ... }
```

I'll go with the Next.js 14 syntax. If user is on Next.js 15, they'd need to await params.

```tsx
"use client";

import { useEditor, EditorContent } from "@tiptap/react";
import StarterKit from "@tiptap/starter-kit";
import { useEffect } from "react";
import {
  Bold,
  Italic,
  List,
  ListOrdered,
  Heading1,
  Heading2,
  Quote,
  Code,
  Strikethrough,
} from "lucide-react";
import { cn } from "@/lib/utils";

interface Props {
  value: string;
  onChange: (val: string) => void;
}

export function Editor({ value, onChange }: Props) {
  const editor = useEditor({
    extensions: [StarterKit],
    content: value || "",
    immediatelyRender: false,
    editorProps: {
      attributes: {
        class:
          "prose prose-sm sm:prose lg:prose-lg focus:outline-none min-h-[300px] max-w-none",
      },
    },
    onUpdate({ editor }) {
      onChange(editor.getHTML());
    },
  });

  useEffect(() => {
    if (editor && value !== editor.getHTML()) {
      editor.commands.setContent(value || "", false);
    }
  }, [value, editor]);

  if (!editor) return null;

  const Btn = ({
    onClick,
    active,
    children,
  }: {
    onClick: () => void;
    active?: boolean;
    children: React.ReactNode;
  }) => (
    <button
      type="button"
      onClick={onClick}
      className={cn(
        "rounded p-1.5 text-gray-600 hover:bg-gray-200",
        active && "bg-gray-200 text-gray-900"
      )}
    >
      {children}
    </button>
  );

  return (
    <div>
      <div className="sticky top-0 z-10 mb-2 flex items-center gap-1 rounded border border-gray-200 bg-white p-1">
        <Btn
          onClick={() => editor.chain().focus().toggleHeading({ level: 1 }).run()}
          active={editor.isActive("heading", { level: 1 })}
        >
          <Heading1 className="h-4 w-4" />
        </Btn>
        <Btn
          onClick={() => editor.chain().focus().toggleHeading({ level: 2 }).run()}
          active={editor.isActive("heading", { level: 2 })}
        >
          <Heading2 className="h-4 w-4" />
        </Btn>
        <Btn
          onClick={() => editor.chain().focus().toggleBold().run()}
          active={editor.isActive("bold")}
        >
          <Bold className="h-4 w-4" />
        </Btn>
        <Btn
          onClick={() => editor.chain().focus().toggleItalic().run()}
          active={editor.isActive("italic")}
        >
          <Italic className="h-4 w-4" />
        </Btn>
        <Btn
          onClick={() => editor.chain().focus().toggleStrike().run()}
          active={editor.isActive("strike")}
        >
          <Strikethrough className="h-4 w-4" />
        </Btn>
        <Btn
          onClick={() => editor.chain().focus().toggleCode().run()}
          active={editor.isActive("code")}
        >
          <Code className="h-4 w-4" />
        </Btn>
        <Btn
          onClick={() => editor.chain().focus().toggleBulletList().run()}
          active={editor.isActive("bulletList")}
        >
          <List className="h-4 w-4" />
        </Btn>
        <Btn
          onClick={() => editor.chain().focus().toggleOrderedList().run()}
          active={editor.isActive("orderedList")}
        >
          <ListOrdered className="h-4 w-4" />
        </Btn>
        <Btn
          onClick={() => editor.chain().focus().toggleBlockquote().run()}
          active={editor.isActive("blockquote")}
        >
          <Quote className="h-4 w-4" />
        </Btn>
      </div>
      <EditorContent editor={editor} />
    </div>
  );
}
```

Below is the complete, runnable Notion-clone MVP. I've split the work into clear layers: **database**, **API**, **layout/UI shell**, **stateful tree**, and **the editor** with debounced auto-save.

## Architecture overview

```
notion-clone/
├── prisma/                       # Database schema
│   └── schema.prisma
├── src/
│   ├── app/                      # Next.js App Router
│   │   ├── api/documents/        # CRUD REST endpoints
│   │   │   ├── route.ts          # GET (list) / POST (create)
│   │   │   └── [id]/route.ts     # GET / PATCH / DELETE
│   │   ├── globals.css
│   │   ├── layout.tsx            # Root layout
│   │   └── page.tsx              # Entry → renders <AppShell/>
│   ├── components/
│   │   ├── AppShell.tsx          # Top-level client shell
│   │   ├── Sidebar.tsx           # Collapsible sidebar
│   │   ├── DocumentTree.tsx      # Recursive tree view
│   │   ├── DocumentEditor.tsx    # Title + editor + auto-save
│   │   ├── Editor.tsx            # TipTap editor + toolbar
│   │   └── EmptyState.tsx
│   ├── hooks/
│   │   └── useDebounce.ts
│   └── lib/
│       ├── prisma.ts             # Prisma singleton
│       ├── tree.ts               # Flat list → tree builder
│       ├── types.ts              # Shared TS types
│       └── utils.ts              # cn() helper
├── .env
├── .env.example
├── .gitignore
├── next.config.js
├── next-env.d.ts
├── package.json
├── postcss.config.js
├── tailwind.config.ts
└── tsconfig.json
```

### Data flow
- `AppShell` fetches **all** documents once via `GET /api/documents`, holds them in state, and derives the tree.
- Selecting a document just sets a `selectedId`; the editor reads its data from the in-memory list.
- The TipTap editor emits HTML on every keystroke. A custom `useDebounce` hook delays 600ms before `PATCH /api/documents/[id]`.
- Tree mutations (create child, delete) call the API and then re-fetch the list.

### Setup

```bash
npm install
npx prisma db push
npm run dev
```

---

## File listings

prisma/schema.prisma
```
generator client {
  provider = "prisma-client-js"
}

datasource db {
  provider = "sqlite"
  url      = env("DATABASE_URL")
}

model Document {
  id               String     @id @default(uuid())
  title            String     @default("Untitled")
  content          String     @default("")
  isPublished      Boolean    @default(false)
  parentDocumentId String?
  parentDocument   Document?  @relation("DocumentHierarchy", fields: [parentDocumentId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  childDocuments   Document[] @relation("DocumentHierarchy")
  createdAt        DateTime   @default(now())
  updatedAt        DateTime   @updatedAt

  @@index([parentDocumentId])
}
```

import { type ClassValue, clsx } from "clsx";
import { twMerge } from "tailwind-merge";

export function cn(...inputs: ClassValue[]) {

}

export interface Document {
  id: string;
  title: string;
  content: string;
  isPublished: boolean;
  parentDocumentId: string | null;

}

export interface DocumentNode extends Document {

src/lib/tree.ts
```
import type { Document, DocumentNode } from "./types";

/**
 * Convert a flat list of documents into a hierarchical tree.
 * Documents whose parent isn't in the list are treated as roots.
 */
export function buildTree(documents: Document[]): DocumentNode[] {
  const map = new Map<string, DocumentNode>();

  documents.forEach((doc) => {
    map.set(doc.id, { ...doc, children: [] });
  });

  const roots: DocumentNode[] = [];

  map.forEach((node) => {
    if (node.parentDocumentId && map.has(node.parentDocumentId)) {
      map.get(node.parentDocumentId)!.children.push(node);
    } else {
      roots.push(node);
    }
  });

  const sortRecursive = (nodes: DocumentNode[]) => {
    nodes.sort((a, b) => a.title.localeCompare(b.title));
    nodes.forEach((n) => sortRecursive(n.children));
  };
  sortRecursive(roots);

  return roots;
}
```

src/hooks/useDebounce.ts
```
"use client";

import { useEffect, useState } from "react";

/**
 * Returns `value` after it has been stable for `delay` ms.
 */
export function useDebounce<T>(value: T, delay = 500): T {
  const [debounced, setDebounced] = useState<T>(value);

  useEffect(() => {
    const t = setTimeout(() => setDebounced(value), delay);
    return () => clearTimeout(t);
  }, [value, delay]);

  return debounced;
}
```

src/app/api/documents/route.ts
```
import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from "next/server";
import { prisma } from "@/lib/prisma";

export async function GET() {
  try {
    const documents = await prisma.document.findMany({
      orderBy: { updatedAt: "desc" },
    });
    return NextResponse.json(documents);
  } catch (error) {
    console.error("[GET /api/documents]", error);
    return NextResponse.json(
      { error: "Failed to fetch documents" },
      { status: 500 }
    );
  }
}

export async function POST(req: NextRequest) {
  try {
    const body = await req.json();
    const { title, content, parentDocumentId, isPublished } = body ?? {};

    const document = await prisma.document.create({
      data: {
        title: typeof title === "string" && title.length > 0 ? title : "Untitled",
        content: typeof content === "string" ? content : "",
        parentDocumentId:
          typeof parentDocumentId === "string" && parentDocumentId.length > 0
            ? parentDocumentId
            : null,
        isPublished: Boolean(isPublished),
      },
    });

    return NextResponse.json(document, { status: 201 });
  } catch (error) {
    console.error("[POST /api/documents]", error);
    return NextResponse.json(
      { error: "Failed to create document" },
      { status: 500 }
    );
  }
}
```

src/app/api/documents/[id]/route.ts
```
import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from "next/server";
import { prisma } from "@/lib/prisma";

type Ctx = { params: { id: string } };

export async function GET(_req: NextRequest, { params }: Ctx) {
  try {
    const document = await prisma.document.findUnique({
      where: { id: params.id },
    });
    if (!document) {
      return NextResponse.json({ error: "Not found" }, { status: 404 });
    }
    return NextResponse.json(document);
  } catch (error) {
    console.error("[GET /api/documents/:id]", error);
    return NextResponse.json(
      { error: "Failed to fetch document" },
      { status: 500 }
    );
  }
}

export async function PATCH(req: NextRequest, { params }: Ctx) {
  try {
    const body = await req.json();
    const data: Record<string, unknown> = {};

    if (typeof body.title === "string") data.title = body.title;
    if (typeof body.content === "string") data.content = body.content;
    if (typeof body.isPublished === "boolean") data.isPublished = body.isPublished;
    if (body.parentDocumentId !== undefined) {
      data.parentDocumentId =
        typeof body.parentDocumentId === "string" && body.parentDocumentId.length > 0
          ? body.parentDocumentId
          : null;
    }

    const document = await prisma.document.update({
      where: { id: params.id },
      data,
    });

    return NextResponse.json(document);
  } catch (error) {
    console.error("[PATCH /api/documents/:id]", error);
    return NextResponse.json(
      { error: "Failed to update document" },
      { status: 500 }
    );
  }
}

export async function DELETE(_req: NextRequest, { params }: Ctx) {
  try {
    await prisma.document.delete({
      where: { id: params.id },
    });
    return NextResponse.json({ success: true });
  } catch (error) {
    console.error("[DELETE /api/documents/:id]", error);
    return NextResponse.json(
      { error: "Failed to delete document" },
      { status: 500 }
    );
  }
}
```

src/components/AppShell.tsx
```
"use client";

import { useCallback, useEffect, useState } from "react";
import { Sidebar } from "./Sidebar";
import { DocumentEditor } from "./DocumentEditor";
import { EmptyState } from "./EmptyState";
import type { Document } from "@/lib/types";

export function AppShell() {
  const [documents, setDocuments] = useState<Document[]>([]);
  const [selectedId, setSelectedId] = useState<string | null>(null);
  const [sidebarOpen, setSidebarOpen] = useState(true);
  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true);

  const fetchDocuments = useCallback(async () => {
    try {
      const res = await fetch("/api/documents", { cache: "no-store" });
      if (!res.ok) throw new Error("Failed to fetch");
      const data: Document[] = await res.json();
      setDocuments(data);
    } catch (err) {
      console.error(err);
    } finally {
      setLoading(false);
    }
  }, []);

  useEffect(() => {
    fetchDocuments();
  }, [fetchDocuments]);

  const createDocument = useCallback(
    async (parentDocumentId: string | null = null) => {
      const res = await fetch("/api/documents", {
        method: "POST",
        headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
        body: JSON.stringify({ title: "Untitled", parentDocumentId }),
      });
      if (!res.ok) return;
      const doc: Document = await res.json();
      await fetchDocuments();
      setSelectedId(doc.id);
    },
    [fetchDocuments]
  );

  const selected = documents.find((d) => d.id === selectedId) ?? null;

  return (
    <div className="flex h-screen w-full overflow-hidden bg-white">
      <Sidebar
        documents={documents}
        selectedId={selectedId}
        onSelect={setSelectedId}
        onCreate={createDocument}
        isOpen={sidebarOpen}
        onToggle={() => setSidebarOpen((v) => !v)}
      />
      <main className="relative flex-1 overflow-hidden">
        {loading ? (
          <div className="flex h-full items-center justify-center text-sm text-gray-400">
            Loading…
          </div>
        ) : selected ? (
          <DocumentEditor
            key={selected.id}
            document={selected}
            onSaved={fetchDocuments}
          />
        ) : (
          <EmptyState onCreate={() => createDocument(null)} />
        )}
      </main>
    </div>
  );
}
```

src/components/Sidebar.tsx
```
"use client";

import {
  ChevronLeft,
  ChevronRight,
  FileText,
  Plus,
  Search,
} from "lucide-react";
import { DocumentTree } from "./DocumentTree";
import type { Document } from "@/lib/types";
import { cn } from "@/lib/utils";

interface Props {
  documents: Document[];
  selectedId: string | null;
  onSelect: (id: string) => void;
  onCreate: (parentId: string | null) => void;
  isOpen: boolean;
  onToggle: () => void;
}

export function Sidebar({
  documents,
  selectedId,
  onSelect,
  onCreate,
  isOpen,
  onToggle,
}: Props) {
  return (
    <aside
      className={cn(
        "flex h-full shrink-0 flex-col border-r border-gray-200 bg-gray-50 transition-[width] duration-200",
        isOpen ? "w-72" : "w-12"
      )}
    >
      <div className="flex h-12 items-center gap-1 border-b border-gray-200 px-2">
        {isOpen && (
          <div className="flex items-center gap-2 px-1 text-sm font-semibold text-gray-700">
            <FileText className="h-4 w-4" />
            <span>My Workspace</span>
          </div>
        )}
        <button
          onClick={onToggle}
          className={cn(
            "rounded p-1.5 text-gray-500 hover:bg-gray-200",
            isOpen ? "ml-auto" : "mx-auto"
          )}
          aria-label="Toggle sidebar"
          title="Toggle sidebar"
        >
          {isOpen ? (
            <ChevronLeft className="h-4 w-4" />
          ) : (
            <ChevronRight className="h-4 w-4" />
          )}
        </button>
      </div>

      {isOpen && (
        <>
          <div className="space-y-1 p-2">
            <button
              onClick={() => onCreate(null)}
              className="flex w-full items-center gap-2 rounded px-2 py-1.5 text-sm text-gray-700 hover:bg-gray-200"
            >
              <Plus className="h-4 w-4" />
              <span>New page</span>
            </button>
            <div className="flex items-center gap-2 rounded px-2 py-1.5 text-sm text-gray-500">
              <Search className="h-4 w-4" />
              <span>Search</span>
            </div>
          </div>

          <div className="px-2 pb-1 pt-1 text-[11px] font-medium uppercase tracking-wider text-gray-400">
            Pages
          </div>
          <div className="flex-1 overflow-y-auto px-1 pb-2">
            <DocumentTree
              documents={documents}
              selectedId={selectedId}
              onSelect={onSelect}
              onCreate={onCreate}
            />
          </div>
        </>
      )}
    </aside>
  );
}
```

src/components/DocumentTree.tsx
```
"use client";

import { useState } from "react";
import {
  ChevronDown,
  ChevronRight,
  FileText,
  MoreHorizontal,
  Plus,
  Trash2,
} from "lucide-react";
import { buildTree } from "@/lib/tree";
import type { DocumentNode, Document } from "@/lib/types";
import { cn } from "@/lib/utils";

interface Props {
  documents: Document[];
  selectedId: string | null;
  onSelect: (id: string) => void;
  onCreate: (parentId: string | null) => void;
}

export function DocumentTree(props: Props) {
  const tree = buildTree(props.documents);

  if (tree.length === 0) {
    return (
      <div className="px-3 py-2 text-xs text-gray-400">No pages yet</div>
    );
  }

  return (
    <ul className="space-y-0.5">
      {tree.map((node) => (
        <TreeNode key={node.id} node={node} depth={0} {...props} />
      ))}
    </ul>
  );
}

interface NodeProps {
  node: DocumentNode;
  depth: number;
  selectedId: string | null;
  onSelect: (id: string) => void;
  onCreate: (parentId: string | null) => void;
}

function TreeNode({ node, depth, selectedId, onSelect, onCreate }: NodeProps) {
  const [open, setOpen] = useState(true);
  const [menuOpen, setMenuOpen] = useState(false);
  const hasChildren = node.children.length > 0;

  const remove = async () => {
    if (!confirm(`Delete "${node.title || "Untitled"}"?`)) return;
    await fetch(`/api/documents/${node.id}`, { method: "DELETE" });
    onCreate; // keep TS happy
    setMenuOpen(false);
    // Trigger a refetch through window event so AppShell can clear selection
    window.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent("documents:changed"));
  };

  return (
    <li>
      <div
        className={cn(
          "group relative flex items-center gap-1 rounded px-1 py-1 text-sm text-gray-700 hover:bg-gray-200",
          selectedId === node.id && "bg-gray-200 font-medium text-gray-900"
        )}
        style={{ paddingLeft: depth * 12 + 4 }}
      >
        <button
          onClick={() => setOpen((v) => !v)}
          className="flex h-4 w-4 items-center justify-center text-gray-500"
          aria-label={open ? "Collapse" : "Expand"}
        >
          {hasChildren ? (
            open ? (
              <ChevronDown className="h-3 w-3" />
            ) : (
              <ChevronRight className="h-3 w-3" />
            )
          ) : (
            <span className="block h-3 w-3" />
          )}
        </button>

        <button
          onClick={() => onSelect(node.id)}
          className="flex flex-1 items-center gap-1.5 truncate text-left"
        >
          <FileText className="h-3.5 w-3.5 shrink-0 text-gray-500" />
          <span className="truncate">{node.title || "Untitled"}</span>
        </button>

        <div className="flex items-center gap-0.5 opacity-0 group-hover:opacity-100">
          <button
            onClick={() => onCreate(node.id)}
            className="rounded p-0.5 text-gray-500 hover:bg-gray-300"
            title="Add sub-page"
          >
            <Plus className="h-3 w-3" />
          </button>
          <div className="relative">
            <button
              onClick={() => setMenuOpen((v) => !v)}
              className="rounded p-0.5 text-gray-500 hover:bg-gray-300"
              title="More"
            >
              <MoreHorizontal className="h-3 w-3" />
            </button>
            {menuOpen && (
              <div
                className="absolute right-0 z-20 mt-1 w-36 rounded-md border border-gray-200 bg-white py-1 text-xs shadow-lg"
                onMouseLeave={() => setMenuOpen(false)}
              >
                <button
                  onClick={() => {
                    setMenuOpen(false);
                    onCreate(node.id);
                  }}
                  className="flex w-full items-center gap-2 px-2 py-1.5 text-left text-gray-700 hover:bg-gray-100"
                >
                  <Plus className="h-3 w-3" /> Add sub-page
                </button>
                <button
                  onClick={remove}
                  className="flex w-full items-center gap-2 px-2 py-1.5 text-left text-red-600 hover:bg-gray-100"
                >
                  <Trash2 className="h-3 w-3" /> Delete
                </button>
              </div>
            )}
          </div>
        </div>
      </div>

      {open && hasChildren && (
        <ul className="space-y-0.5">
          {node.children.map((child) => (
            <TreeNode
              key={child.id}
              node={child}
              depth={depth + 1}
              selectedId={selectedId}
              onSelect={onSelect}
              onCreate={onCreate}
            />
          ))}
        </ul>
      )}
    </li>
  );
}
```

src/components/DocumentEditor.tsx
```
"use client";

import { useEffect, useRef, useState } from "react";
import { Editor } from "./Editor";
import { useDebounce } from "@/hooks/useDebounce";
import type { Document } from "@/lib/types";
import { Trash2 } from "lucide-react";

interface Props {
  document: Document;
  onSaved: () => void;
}

type SaveStatus = "saved" | "saving" | "unsaved" | "error";

export function DocumentEditor({ document, onSaved }: Props) {
  const [title, setTitle] = useState(document.title);
  const [content, setContent] = useState(document.content);
  const [status, setStatus] = useState<SaveStatus>("saved");
  const lastSavedRef = useRef({ title: document.title, content: document.content });

  // Reset local state when the selected document changes
  useEffect(() => {
    setTitle(document.title);
    setContent(document.content);
    setStatus("saved");
    lastSavedRef.current = { title: document.title, content: document.content };
  }, [document.id]); // eslint-disable-line react-hooks/exhaustive-deps

  const debouncedTitle = useDebounce(title, 700);
  const debouncedContent = useDebounce(content, 700);

  useEffect(() => {
    const last = lastSavedRef.current;
    if (debouncedTitle === last.title && debouncedContent === last.content) {
      return;
    }

    let cancelled = false;
    setStatus("saving");

    fetch(`/api/documents/${document.id}`, {
      method: "PATCH",
      headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
      body: JSON.stringify({
        title: debouncedTitle,
        content: debouncedContent,
      }),
    })
      .then((res) => {
        if (!res.ok) throw new Error("Save failed");
        if (cancelled) return;
        lastSavedRef.current = { title: debouncedTitle, content: debouncedContent };
        setStatus("saved");
        onSaved();
      })
      .catch(() => {
        if (!cancelled) setStatus("error");
      });

    return () => {
      cancelled = true;
    };
  }, [debouncedTitle, debouncedContent, document.id, onSaved]);

  const remove = async () => {
    if (!confirm(`Delete "${title || "Untitled"}"?`)) return;
    await fetch(`/api/documents/${document.id}`, { method: "DELETE" });
    window.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent("documents:changed"));
  };

  return (
    <div className="mx-auto flex h-full max-w-3xl flex-col">
      <div className="flex items-center justify-between px-12 pt-10">
        <div className="text-xs text-gray-400">
          {status === "saving" && "Saving…"}
          {status === "saved" && "Saved"}
          {status === "unsaved" && "Unsaved changes"}
          {status === "error" && (
            <span className="text-red-500">Failed to save</span>
          )}
        </div>
        <button
          onClick={remove}
          className="rounded p-1.5 text-gray-400 hover:bg-gray-100 hover:text-red-500"
          title="Delete page"
        >
          <Trash2 className="h-4 w-4" />
        </button>
      </div>

      <input
        value={title}
        onChange={(e) => {
          setTitle(e.target.value);
          setStatus("unsaved");
        }}
        placeholder="Untitled"
        className="mx-12 mb-4 mt-2 bg-transparent text-4xl font-bold text-gray-900 outline-none placeholder:text-gray-300"
      />

      <div className="flex-1 overflow-y-auto px-12 pb-12">
        <Editor value={content} onChange={(v) => { setContent(v); setStatus("unsaved"); }} />
      </div>
    </div>
  );
}
```

src/components/Editor.tsx
```
"use client";

import { useEffect } from "react";
import { useEditor, EditorContent } from "@tiptap/react";
import StarterKit from "@tiptap/starter-kit";
import Placeholder from "@tiptap/extension-placeholder";
import {
  Bold,
  Code,
  Heading1,
  Heading2,
  Italic,
  List,
  ListOrdered,
  Quote,
  Strikethrough,
} from "lucide-react";
import { cn } from "@/lib/utils";

interface Props {
  value: string;
  onChange: (html: string) => void;
}

export function Editor({ value, onChange }: Props) {
  const editor = useEditor({
    extensions: [
      StarterKit.configure({
        heading: { levels: [1, 2, 3] },
      }),
      Placeholder.configure({
        placeholder: "Type '/' for commands, or just start writing…",
      }),
    ],
    content: value || "",
    immediatelyRender: false, // important for Next.js SSR
    editorProps: {
      attributes: {
        class:
          "prose prose-sm sm:prose lg:prose-lg max-w-none min-h-[400px] focus:outline-none",
      },
    },
    onUpdate({ editor }) {
      onChange(editor.getHTML());
    },
  });

  // Sync external value changes (e.g. document switch) into the editor
  useEffect(() => {
    if (!editor) return;
    if (value !== editor.getHTML()) {
      editor.commands.setContent(value || "", false);
    }
  }, [value, editor]);

  if (!editor) {
    return (
      <div className="h-[400px] animate-pulse rounded bg-gray-50" />
    );
  }

  const tools: Array<{
    icon: React.ComponentType<{ className?: string }>;
    label: string;
    run: () => void;
    active: boolean;
  }> = [
    {
      icon: Heading1,
      label: "Heading 1",
      run: () => editor.chain().focus().toggleHeading({ level: 1 }).run(),
      active: editor.isActive("heading", { level: 1 }),
    },
    {
      icon: Heading2,
      label: "Heading 2",
      run: () => editor.chain().focus().toggleHeading({ level: 2 }).run(),
      active: editor.isActive("heading", { level: 2 }),
    },
    {
      icon: Bold,
      label: "Bold",
      run: () => editor.chain().focus().toggleBold().run(),
      active: editor.isActive("bold"),
    },
    {
      icon: Italic,
      label: "Italic",
      run: () => editor.chain().focus().toggleItalic().run(),
      active: editor.isActive("italic"),
    },
    {
      icon: Strikethrough,
      label: "Strike",
      run: () => editor.chain().focus().toggleStrike().run(),
      active: editor.isActive("strike"),
    },
    {
      icon: Code,
      label: "Inline code",
      run: () => editor.chain().focus().toggleCode().run(),
      active: editor.isActive("code"),
    },
    {
      icon: List,
      label: "Bulleted list",
      run: () => editor.chain().focus().toggleBulletList().run(),
      active: editor.isActive("bulletList"),
    },
    {
      icon: ListOrdered,
      label: "Numbered list",
      run: () => editor.chain().focus().toggleOrderedList().run(),
      active: editor.isActive("orderedList"),
    },
    {
      icon: Quote,
      label: "Quote",
      run: () => editor.chain().focus().toggleBlockquote().run(),
      active: editor.isActive("blockquote"),
    },
  ];

  return (
    <div>
      <div className="sticky top-0 z-10 mb-2 flex flex-wrap items-center gap-0.5 rounded-md border border-gray-200 bg-white/80 p-1 backdrop-blur">
        {tools.map((t) => (
          <button
            key={t.label}
            type="button"
            onClick={t.run}
            title={t.label}
            className={cn(
              "rounded p-1.5 text-gray-600 hover:bg-gray-100",
              t.active && "bg-gray-100 text-gray-900"
            )}
          >
            <t.icon className="h-4 w-4" />
          </button>
        ))}
      </div>
      <EditorContent editor={editor} />
    </div>
  );
}
```

"use client";

import { FileText } from "lucide-react";

interface Props {
  onCreate: () => void;
}

export function EmptyState({ onCreate }: Props) {
  return (
    <div className="flex h-full flex-col items-center justify-center px-6 text-center text-gray-400">
      <FileText className="mb-4 h-12 w-12" />
      <h2 className="mb-1 text-lg font-semibold text-gray-700">
        No page selected
      </h2>
      <p className="mb-5 max-w-sm text-sm">
        Pick a page from the sidebar, or create a new one to get started.
      </p>
      <button
        onClick={onCreate}
        className="rounded-md bg-blue-600 px-4 py-2 text-sm font-medium text-white shadow-sm hover:bg-blue-700"
      >
        + New page
      </button>
    </div>
  );
}
```

---

## What you get

- **Database**: a `Document` model with self-referencing `parentDocumentId` for nested pages. Cascade-delete ensures children disappear with their parent.
- **API** (`/api/documents`):
  - `GET` — list all
  - `POST` — create (title, optional parent, optional content)
  - `GET /:id` — fetch one
  - `PATCH /:id` — partial update (title/content/published/parent)
  - `DELETE /:id` — remove
- **UI shell**:
  - Collapsible sidebar with a `+ New page` button, search affordance, and a recursive `DocumentTree` (collapse/expand, hover-only add + delete menu, hierarchical indent).
  - Empty state with a "create new page" CTA.
  - Editor view: huge editable title, a TipTap toolbar (H1, H2, bold, italic, strike, inline code, bullet/numbered lists, blockquote), and the rich-text body.
- **Auto-save**: both the title input and editor emit updates; `useDebounce` (700 ms) coalesces keystrokes into a single `PATCH`. A status line shows `Saving… / Saved / Failed to save`.
- **Document switching**: `<DocumentEditor key={selected.id} />` fully remounts when you pick a different page, so the editor state is always clean.

### Quick start
```bash
npm install              # also runs `prisma generate`
npx prisma db push       # creates ./dev.db with the schema
npm run dev              # http://localhost:3000
```

The MVP is fully functional end-to-end. From here you can layer on: cover images, drag-to-reorder, slash commands, sharing, publishing, authentication, etc.

Below is the complete Notion-clone MVP codebase rewritten strictly in Aider's file-listing format. Every file is listed in full — no truncation, no comments like "rest of code…", and each listing starts with a bare filename on its first line.

## Architecture overview

```
notion-clone/
├── prisma/schema.prisma
├── src/
│   ├── app/
│   │   ├── api/documents/route.ts
│   │   ├── api/documents/[id]/route.ts
│   │   ├── globals.css
│   │   ├── layout.tsx
│   │   └── page.tsx
│   ├── components/
│   │   ├── AppShell.tsx
│   │   ├── Sidebar.tsx
│   │   ├── DocumentTree.tsx
│   │   ├── DocumentEditor.tsx
│   │   ├── Editor.tsx
│   │   └── EmptyState.tsx
│   ├── hooks/useDebounce.ts
│   └── lib/
│       ├── prisma.ts
│       ├── tree.ts
│       ├── types.ts
│       └── utils.ts
├── .env
├── .env.example
├── .gitignore
├── next.config.js
├── next-env.d.ts
├── package.json
├── postcss.config.js
├── tailwind.config.ts
└── tsconfig.json
```

### Setup

```bash
npm install
npx prisma db push
npm run dev
```

---

## File listings

prisma/schema.prisma
```
generator client {
  provider = "prisma-client-js"
}

datasource db {
  provider = "sqlite"
  url      = env("DATABASE_URL")
}

model Document {
  id               String     @id @default(uuid())
  title            String     @default("Untitled")
  content          String     @default("")
  isPublished      Boolean    @default(false)
  parentDocumentId String?
  parentDocument   Document?  @relation("DocumentHierarchy", fields: [parentDocumentId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  childDocuments   Document[] @relation("DocumentHierarchy")
  createdAt        DateTime   @default(now())
  updatedAt        DateTime   @updatedAt

  @@index([parentDocumentId])
}
```

.env
```
DATABASE_URL="file:./dev.db"
```

.env.example
```
DATABASE_URL="file:./dev.db"
```

.gitignore
```
node_modules
.next
out
dist
.env
.env.local
dev.db
dev.db-journal
*.log
.DS_Store
```

{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "target": "ES2020",
    "lib": ["dom", "dom.iterable", "esnext"],
    "allowJs": true,
    "skipLibCheck": true,
    "strict": true,
    "noEmit": true,
    "esModuleInterop": true,
    "module": "esnext",
    "moduleResolution": "bundler",
    "resolveJsonModule": true,
    "isolatedModules": true,
    "jsx": "preserve",
    "incremental": true,
    "plugins": [{ "name": "next" }],
    "paths": {
      "@/*": ["./src/*"]
    }
  },
  "include": ["next-env.d.ts", "**/*.ts", "**/*.tsx", ".next/types/**/*.ts"],
  "exclude": ["node_modules"]
}

src/lib/prisma.ts
```
import { PrismaClient } from "@prisma/client";

const globalForPrisma = globalThis as unknown as {
  prisma: PrismaClient | undefined;
};

export const prisma =
  globalForPrisma.prisma ??
  new PrismaClient({
    log:
      process.env.NODE_ENV === "development" ? ["error", "warn"] : ["error"],
  });

if (process.env.NODE_ENV !== "production") globalForPrisma.prisma = prisma;
```

src/lib/utils.ts
```
import { type ClassValue, clsx } from "clsx";
import { twMerge } from "tailwind-merge";

export function cn(...inputs: ClassValue[]) {
  return twMerge(clsx(inputs));
}
```

export interface Document {
  id: string;
  title: string;
  content: string;
  isPublished: boolean;
  parentDocumentId: string | null;

}

export interface DocumentNode extends Document {

src/lib/tree.ts
```
import type { Document, DocumentNode } from "./types";

/**
 * Convert a flat list of documents into a hierarchical tree.
 * Documents whose parent isn't in the list are treated as roots.
 */
export function buildTree(documents: Document[]): DocumentNode[] {
  const map = new Map<string, DocumentNode>();

  documents.forEach((doc) => {
    map.set(doc.id, { ...doc, children: [] });
  });

  const roots: DocumentNode[] = [];

  map.forEach((node) => {
    if (node.parentDocumentId && map.has(node.parentDocumentId)) {
      map.get(node.parentDocumentId)!.children.push(node);
    } else {
      roots.push(node);
    }
  });

  const sortRecursive = (nodes: DocumentNode[]) => {
    nodes.sort((a, b) => a.title.localeCompare(b.title));
    nodes.forEach((n) => sortRecursive(n.children));
  };
  sortRecursive(roots);

  return roots;
}
```

src/hooks/useDebounce.ts
```
"use client";

import { useEffect, useState } from "react";

/**
 * Returns `value` after it has been stable for `delay` ms.
 */
export function useDebounce<T>(value: T, delay = 500): T {
  const [debounced, setDebounced] = useState<T>(value);

  useEffect(() => {
    const t = setTimeout(() => setDebounced(value), delay);
    return () => clearTimeout(t);
  }, [value, delay]);

  return debounced;
}
```
